reasoning: Copernicus ERA5 data for October 2025 showed it being 0.16°C cooler than the record-breaking October 2023. October 2023 had a GISTEMP anomaly of ~1.34°C. Applying a similar delta to GISTEMP yields an expected October 2025 anomaly of roughly 1.34 - 0.16 = 1.18°C. This calculation puts the likely GISTEMP release for October 2025 slightly above the 1.10°C to 1.14°C bracket. Given the typical historical error margins in predicting GISTEMP from ERA5 (~0.03-0.05°C), the 1.10-1.14°C range is within the realm of possibility but sits below the central estimate. Thus, the probability of the final NASA value landing exactly in this specific 0.05°C-wide bracket is relatively low, estimated at around 15%.
